novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: Computation upgrade is only partial: the fixed-slice flow theorem is explicit, but the flagship finite strongly-polynomial endpoint search still ranges over an undefined/possibly continuous retained-mass set; delta_summary promises full coordinate endpoint computation.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The headline constructive object is a finite candidate-ratio/retained-mass search algorithm, but §9 computes only one fixed-slice endpoint and does not exhibit Λ_j, the search table, or all coordinate endpoints on the worked instance.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The active-face dual map is promised, but §9 gives only a partial fixed-slice certificate and does not compute dual multipliers that identify the active neighborhood envelope and cell cap.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The multi-cutoff comparators in the Results-at-a-glance gap are cited mostly by bibkey/abstract-level description; a flagship computational extension needs section/page/equation anchors for the precise missing many-neighborhood manipulation-bound problem.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The proposed search is finite only in λ; the retained-mass profile set M_j is not made finite or given a strongly-polynomial separation/optimization primitive, so the stated finite parametric search is not mathematically pinned down.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Coordinate endpoints are defined over the full nested class Z(P,rho_bar,s_bar), but the search is stated for prefix neighborhood j and does not explicitly enforce extendability to later neighborhoods l>j.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: A fixed-m lifted-flow dual cannot by itself identify active envelope and cell-cap constraints, because those constraints govern the choice of m rather than the fixed-m flow problem unless they are included in the network/dual.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: §10 names the parametric search as a substantive step but gives no concrete algebraic route or classical theorem explaining why O(M_j^2) candidate ratios plus M_j-search suffice.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace M_j by an explicit finite retained-mass candidate set, or state a separation/optimization oracle for m with a strong-polynomial complexity bound independent of capacity magnitudes.
Expand Exhibit 9.1 into a full endpoint-search table: list Λ_j, feasible retained-mass candidates, flow optima, dual values, and all B_{j,L}, B_{j,U} for the small instance.
Add extension constraints or a projection lemma showing that each prefix fixed-slice solution extends to a full J-neighborhood nested retained plan.
Move envelope/cell-cap constraints into the optimization whose dual is being certified, or downgrade Conjecture 2 to a fixed-flow reduced-cost certificate without active budget interpretation.

recommended_next_step: Revise the computation kernel so the finite endpoint algorithm and active-budget dual are defined and exhibited from primitives; current tier is field, below novelty_target=flagship.

upgrade_review.upgrade_delta_one_line: The fixed-slice min-cost-flow delta is delivered, but the promised full strongly-polynomial coordinate-endpoint computation remains under-specified because the retained-mass search and active-budget dual are not yet finite/constructive.
